events.title: A transcription finished
events.description: Emitted in-band on the response to a transcription request: scribe returns the resulting text (plus a cleanup outcome when an LLM cleanup pass ran). Scribe is stateless — it does not fan this out to a vault or any subscriber; the caller (e.g. a vault's transcription worker) owns persisting the result. There is no hub-mediated webhook for this event today.

actions.title: Transcribe audio
actions.description: Turn an audio file into text (Whisper-compatible), optionally running an LLM cleanup pass. This is a SYNCHRONOUS request-sink, not a webhook: the caller POSTs the audio bytes as multipart/form-data to /scribe/v1/audio/transcriptions (or a public URL to /scribe/v1/audio/transcriptions-url) and reads {text} off the response. It is deliberately NOT a hub `vault-trigger` connection — scribe does not accept a per-note webhook payload and never reads from or writes to a vault. The vault↔scribe handoff is driven vault-side: a vault's in-process transcription worker reads the audio attachment and calls this endpoint when the vault's `auto_transcribe` is enabled. Carries no `provision` block by design, so the hub Connections engine correctly declines to wire it as a webhook trigger.

inputSchema.description: multipart/form-data on /v1/audio/transcriptions (Whisper API shape), or JSON on /v1/audio/transcriptions-url.

file.description: The audio file (wav/mp3/m4a/ogg) — multipart endpoint only.

url.description: A public URL to fetch the audio from — JSON (-url) endpoint only.

cleanup.description: Override the configured cleanup default for this request ('true'/'false'/'1'/'0').

context.description: Optional per-request proper-nouns / glossary block the cleanup pass corrects against. Supplied by the caller, never stored by scribe.

connectionTemplates.title: Auto-transcribe a vault's audio
connectionTemplates.description: Link scribe to a Parachute vault so audio notes recorded in that vault are transcribed automatically. The operator picks WHICH vault (the parameter); scribe's config UI enables auto-transcription in that vault with the operator's approval (the click is the approval). This is a CONFIG link, not a hub `vault-trigger` connection: the vault's own transcription worker calls scribe directly over loopback (discovered via services.json) when its `auto_transcribe` toggle is on — there is no per-note webhook and scribe holds no vault credentials. Enabling the link mints a short-lived `vault:<vault>:admin` token from the hub (cookie-gated to the logged-in operator) and flips the chosen vault's `auto_transcribe.enabled`.

parameters.title: Vault
parameters.description: Which vault's audio notes scribe should transcribe — the operator chooses from the hub's installed vaults.
